mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368233M as of 2006-08-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative average forward returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-08-01 indicates restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC is negative and statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 0.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135500K as of 2006-08-01 represents near-peak employment
failure_condition: When PAYEMS declines > 0.5% MoM, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal demand-side momentum. When both series show positive 3-month growth simultaneously, the combined signal of broad-based demand strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures non-linear amplification of demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1795K and RSAFS at 368233M both elevated in mid-2006
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS momentum) with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year window, or joint positive growth periods produce negative returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling supply-side weakness and potential profit margin compression. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors react to near-term earnings revision risk.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth falls below 2% annualized, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) and nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) interact to signal labor market tightness. When UNRATE is below 5.0% AND PAYEMS growth is positive, tight labor conditions support wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS at 135500K indicate tight labor market in 2006
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction (UNRATE < 5.0% AND PAYEMS growth > 0) with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or joint tight labor conditions produce negative returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 5.5%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the Fed is forced to tighten further, compressing valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-08-01
failure_condition: When inflation > 3.0% YoY and FEDFUNDS < 5.5%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations and recession probability increases.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11397.6B (Q2 2006) reflects moderating growth trajectory
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
